updated_conclusion: The worker publish callback at 0x14066F3F0 appends wrapper+0x8 into a parent-owned active-entry vector at parent+0xE0 by calling 0x1401369D0. Helper 0x1401369D0 is a 0x10-byte ref-counted-pair vector append/grow routine. The cleanup path at 0x14066D7B4 walks the same parent+0xE0 vector, clears each active object's +0x28/+0x30 pair, invokes the object vtable +0x20 hook, removes the object's keyed record from the parent+0x70 sorted 0x18-byte record vector, then resets parent+0xE8 back to parent+0xE0.

tooling_milestone.change: Extended the native PE instruction decoder for XORPS and one-operand F7 group instructions such as IMUL r/m64.
tooling_milestone.why_it_matters: The active-entry append, drain, lower_bound, and erase helpers now decode with zero unknown instructions through Reverser's native pe-instructions exporter instead of requiring another one-off Ghidra dump.

observed_behavior: Treats RCX as a vector header with begin at +0x0, end at +0x8, and capacity end at +0x10.
Treats RDX as the source 0x10-byte pair. The first qword is ref-counted; the second qword is copied as the companion payload/object pointer.
Fast path compares end with capacity end, advances the end pointer by 0x10, copies both qwords into the old end slot, and increments [first_qword+0x8] when the copied first qword is non-null.

Grow path computes the old element count from (end - begin) / 0x10, chooses capacity 1 for empty vectors or twice the old count for non-empty vectors, allocates capacity*0x10 bytes through 0x140793B40, migrates old pairs into the new buffer while zeroing old slots, appends the source pair, and increments the source first-qword refcount when non-null.

After migration, calls 0x1400310A0(begin,end) to clean the old range, releases the old backing buffer through allocator state at 0x140E57B40 via CALL R10 when begin was non-null, then updates begin/end/capacity in the vector header.

ordered_calls.role: Allocate the new backing store for the grow path.
Clean the old migrated pair range after old slots have been zeroed.
Release the previous backing buffer through allocator/global state at 0x140E57B40.

observed_behavior: Iterates the active-entry vector at parent+0xE0..parent+0xE8 in 0x10-byte entries; the active object is read from entry+0x8.
For each active object, validates or clears the object's +0x30 pair through 0x1407B5A50 when the pair looks populated, then zeros the pair storage.
Moves old object+0x28 into a local, zeros object+0x28 and object+0x30, and releases the old ref-counted pointer through vtable +0x8/+0x10 when its interlocked counts drop to one.
Calls the active object's vtable +0x20 hook.

Locks parent+0x20, derives a dword key from object+0x20, lower_bounds the parent+0x70 sorted record vector through 0x14066E300, erases a matching 0x18-byte record through 0x14066DD60, and unlocks parent+0x20.

After all entries have been processed, calls 0x1400310A0 over the parent+0xE0..parent+0xE8 active-entry range and resets parent+0xE8 to parent+0xE0 before tail-jumping to the parent+0x90 unlock helper 0x1407B5260.

ordered_calls.role: Validate/clear populated object+0x30 pair before zeroing it.
Ref-counted release of the old object+0x28 pointer.
Per-entry object hook before removing the keyed record.
Lock parent+0x20 before touching the parent+0x70 sorted records.
Lower_bound lookup in parent+0x70 using the object's dword key at +0x20.
Unlock parent+0x20 after the per-entry sorted-record removal.
Clean the processed active-entry range before resetting the vector end pointer.

active_entry_vector_drain_0x14066d7b4.role_name: active-entry drain and keyed-record cleanup helper

observed_behavior: Treats RCX as a vector header for 0x18-byte records and RDX as a pointer to a dword key.
Computes record count from (end - begin) / 0x18 using multiply by 0x2AAAAAAAAAAAAAAB and shift adjustment.
Binary searches records by the dword at record+0x0 and returns the lower_bound pointer in RAX.
Treats RCX as the parent+0x70 vector header and RDX as the record to erase.
If records follow the erased slot, repeatedly moves each following 0x18-byte record left through 0x14034FF80.
Decrements the vector end by 0x18 and calls 0x14002FDE0 on the old final record storage.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-callback-wrapper-and-native-instr: Completes the previously named 0x14066F3F0 publish callsite by proving 0x1401369D0 is the parent+0xE0 active-entry vector append helper.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-sorted-vector-helper-map: Connects the parent+0x70 sorted vector to the active-entry drain path and identifies the exact lower_bound and erase helpers used to remove keyed records.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.reverser-tooling: Turns two more manual decode gaps into native pe-instructions support so short helper bodies remain repeatable JSON exports.

next_targets: Trace scheduler/state helpers 0x1407A4620, 0x1407A6630, and 0x1407A6990 to refine the payload state machine inside 0x14066F3F0.
Trace provider/object constructors 0x14066D010, 0x1400D24B0, and 0x1400D1990 to connect construction-time labels to the objects later published through parent+0xE0.
Search other direct callers of 0x1401369D0 for shared vector-type reuse patterns and confirm whether all callers use the same 0x10-byte ref-counted pair layout.
